description.en: Bushy, erect, spiny perennial solanaceous shrub valued both as a specialty vegetable (green berries used in *thibbatu mallung*) and as a **resistant rootstock for grafting eggplant/tomato** for bacterial-wilt management in Sri Lanka. Currently under-utilised but recognised for its functional (Ayurvedic) benefits; one Mannar-District farmer produces ~6,000 kg/yr for organic specialty supermarket channels. Hardy, drought-tolerant, grows readily on marginal land with minimal inputs.

description.quote: Solanum torvum (thibbatu), also known as turkey berry, devil's fig, pea eggplant, platebrush or susumber, is a bushy, erect and spiny perennial plant used horticulturally as a rootstock for eggplant. A farmer in Mannar District is promoting thibbatu as a specialist organic vegetable, with plans for producing some 6,000 kg per year
